articles.title: Pro-Democrat group sues to halt UFC fights on White House lawn on Trump's birthday
articles.description: A Democrat-leaning group is suing to stop the UFC Freedom 250 fights at the White House next weekend, arguing that it is a “profoundly corrupt” event in which President Trump and his political allies will gain financially. The lawsuit by the Public Integrity Project, a nonprofit legal organization that aligns itself ...

articles.title: Iran Cancels All Flights After Fresh Israel Face-Off; Triggers Fears Of War Resumption
articles.description: Flights across Iran were grounded after new military exchanges with Israel, as tensions surged and fears of a wider Middle East conflict grew.

articles.title: Life lesson of the day by John D. Rockefeller: “Don’t be afraid to give up the good to go for the great.” — Why do some people achieve more than others despite similar opportunities? Learn from the man who built one of history’s greatest business empires through vision, discipline, and relentless growth
articles.description: Life Lesson of the Day by John D. Rockefeller offers a powerful success mindset for modern life. Rockefeller built one of historys largest business fortunes by making bold decisions when others chose comfort. His famous quote reveals a timeless truth. Personal growth, leadership success, wealth creation, and life-changing opportunities often begin when people leave good situations for greater possibilities. The lesson is simple yet profound. Growth rarely comes from safety. Greatness often starts with one courageous choice.

articles.title: Mumbaikars call out jewellery stores for judging outfits: 'Bought earrings worth Rs 1 lakh'
articles.description: Mumbai shoppers share viral posts alleging appearance-based bias at jewellery stores, saying staff behaviour and service levels often shift depending on how customers dress rather than what they intend to buy.

articles.title: The Inside Story Of Rivalry Between Khan Sir And Raushan Sir
articles.description: Khan Sir and Raushan Anand of Gyan Bindu Coaching in Patna

articles.title: Why Upendra Kushwaha’s son Deepak Prakash may lose his ministerial berth
articles.description: Deepak Prakash MLC Ticket Denial: Bihar Minister Deepak Prakash faces exit after NDA denies him a Legislative Council ticket amid BJP-RLM tension.

articles.title: UP police constable recruitment exam 2025 conducted peacefully across 75 districts; Over 7.23 lakh candidates appear
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh police constable recruitment exams concluded smoothly statewide on Monday, with over 7.23 lakh candidates appearing. Elaborate security and monitoring were in place to prevent malpractice. Authorities registered cases against individuals spreading false information and attempting unfair means, including two arrests for possessing mobile phones during the exam. Strict action is promised against any compromise to the recruitment process.

articles.title: Russia to Back Bangladesh's BRICS Bid After Pause on Membership Expansion Ends: FM Lavrov
articles.description: Russia will support Bangladesh's bid to join BRICS once the pause on the group's expansion is lifted, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Monday.

articles.title: 'US Won't Hesitate...': Trump's Final Warning? Iran Faces Uranium Red Line As Deal Hangs In Balance
articles.description: Donald Trump has issued a stark message to Tehran, declaring that Iran’s highly enriched uranium will be destroyed either through a negotiated deal or through military action. In a revealing interview, Trump claimed Washington and Tehran are close to an agreement but warned that the U.S. would act forcefully if talks collapse. He also signaled openness to engaging with Iran’s new leadership while insisting that Tehran must never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons. The comments offer a rare glimpse into the high-stakes diplomacy shaping the future of the Iran conflict.

articles.title: India expands nuclear arsenal to 190 warheads, pulls further ahead of Pakistan: SIPRI
articles.description: India's nuclear arsenal has risen to an estimated 190 warheads, widening its lead over Pakistan, according to SIPRI Yearbook 2026.

articles.title: Iran fired 30 missiles at Israel since Sunday night
articles.description: Iran launched almost 30 ballistic missiles at Israel starting Sunday night. This marks the first direct confrontation between the two nations since a ceasefire in April. Houthi rebels from Yemen also fired two missiles towards Israel. The Israeli military confirmed the missile exchanges. This escalation signals a significant development in regional tensions.

articles.title: India’s birth rate is falling, but why is Elon Musk worried?
articles.description: According to the United Nations Population Fund’s State of World Population Report 2025, India’s total fertility rate (TFR) currently stands at 1.9 births per woman. This is below the replacement level of 2.1 required to maintain a stable population over time. But why are some, including billionaire Elon Musk, worried?

articles.description: The Indian government plans to introduce a Delimitation Bill during the Monsoon session to increase Lok Sabha seats and redraw constituency boundaries.

articles.title: Consensual teen relationship can’t be prosecuted under adult POCSO trial: Madras High Court
articles.description: The Madras High Court quashed a POCSO case against a young man, holding that a child in conflict with law must be dealt with under the Juvenile Justice Act.

articles.title: Massive crisis for Mamata: TMC MPs meet BJP's Bhupender Yadav as revolt deepens
articles.description: Hours after resigning from the party and the Rajya Sabha, former TMC MP Sukhendu Sekhar Roy reached Union Minister Bhupender Yadav’s residence along with five TMC MPs.

articles.title: Kerala-born Nithya Raman secures second place in Los Angeles mayoral primary
articles.description: Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman secures second place in the LA mayoral primary election, surpassing Spencer Pratt to challenge incumbent Karen Bass in November.

articles.title: Nehru Addressed 3 Foreign Parliaments, Modi 19: What It Says About India’s Rising Global Voice
articles.description: PM Modi’s 19 foreign parliament addresses, the highest by any Indian executive head, reflect India’s expanding diplomatic reach across world capitals.

articles.title: Brother-in-law resigned to avoid nepotism controversy: Kerala Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph
articles.description: Kerala Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph confirmed his brother-in-law Benny Thomas resigned as personal staff. Thomas stepped down following opposition claims of nepotism. Joseph stated Thomas's public standing was strong and his colleagues supported his continuation. Thomas resigned due to the controversy. Joseph needed a trusted aide for his key portfolios.

articles.title: Why Vijay-led TVK is not attending key INDIA bloc meeting in Delhi
articles.description: While Congress joined hands with TVK after the Tamil Nadu Assembly election and supports the Vijay government, this understanding is limited to Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: China’s nuclear arsenal is growing faster than any other country: What it means for India’s security calculus
articles.description: China's nuclear stockpile has risen to 620 warheads, but SIPRI indicates that its expanding missile infrastructure, increasing nuclear readiness and military modernisation may matter more than the increase in warhead numbers alone.

articles.title: NCP Sidesteps Chhagan Bhujbal, Sends Rajendra Jain To Rajya Sabha
articles.description: NCP's decision to nominate Rajendra Jain to the Rajya Sabha seat vacated by Sunetra Pawar has reignited discussion over the party's internal dynamics, leadership priorities and Chhagan Bhujbal's role within the organisation.

articles.title: PM Modi’s upcoming Jakarta visit in focus as Jaishankar pushes deeper India-Indonesia ties
articles.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar and Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono co-chaired the 8th India-Indonesia Joint Commission Meeting in New Delhi, where both sides reviewed cooperation across defence, maritime security, trade and regional issues. 'Our Comprehensive Strategic Partnership has witnessed a strong growth in recent years,' Jaishankar said, adding that the two countries also exchanged views on regional developments, multilateral coordination and deeper India-ASEAN engagement.#IndiaIndonesia #SJaishankar #Indonesia #PMModi #IndiaASEAN #Diplomacy #ForeignPolicy #MaritimeSecurity #DefenceCooperation #IndoPacific #Geopolitics #IndiaNews #InternationalRelations #MEA #Jakarta

articles.description: Subhash C Kashyap made India’s Constitution and Parliament easier to understand. His books remain essential guides on constitutional law democracy governance and parliamentary traditions

articles.description: Khan, a close aide of TMC national general secretary and Lok Sabha MP Abhishek Banerjee, was the TMC candidate from the Falta assembly constituency for the West Bengal Assembly polls.

articles.description: Hong Kong proposes legislation allowing the city leader to define national security offenses, intensifying control over civil liberties.

articles.title: Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner’s Albania luxury resort plan sparks massive protests - What we know so far
articles.description: Protests erupt in Albania against a massive luxury resort planned by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump. Thousands demonstrate against the $6.1 billion project on protected coastal land. Environmental groups warn of ecosystem damage and habitat loss. The Albanian government supports the investment, calling it crucial for the nation. The controversy highlights a clash between development and conservation.

articles.title: Cuba prepares for war while US can’t keep up with losses from Iranian proxies? Big details out
articles.description: Are the U.S. and Cuba heading toward their most dangerous confrontation in decades? Reports claim Havana is preparing civilians for potential conflict as Washington ramps up sanctions and pressure on Cuba’s leadership. President Donald Trump recently suggested Cuba has “sort of collapsed,” while new sanctions targeted top Cuban officials and relatives of former leader Raul Castro. Meanwhile, Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel accused the U.S. of waging economic warfare and trying to force national collapse. As tensions escalate across the Caribbean, fears of a new Cuba crisis are growing rapidly.

articles.title: Who Is Winning The US-Iran War? 100 Days On, The Answer Is Complicated
articles.description: As the Iran-US war completes 100 days, the conflict has strengthened some players, exposed others, and turned into a test of endurance across the Middle East.

articles.title: Actor Sukanya wins defamation case against Sun TV after 30 years over Veerappan interview, Madras HC asks TV to pay Rs 10 lakh damage
articles.description: Actor Sukanya has won a 30-year defamation case after the Madras High Court ordered Sun TV to pay Rs 10 lakh for airing Veerappan’s scandalous claims without verification.

articles.title: China's Xi set to visit North Korea for closely watched talks with Kim Jong Un
articles.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is set to travel to Pyongyang on Monday in his first visit in 7 years, likely to reassert China’s unique influence over North Korea

articles.title: Middle East on edge: Iran and Israel trade fresh blows despite Trump's warning; how the sudden escalation unfolded
articles.description: The strikes could also derailed ​​United States presdient Donald Trump's last-ditch effort to find an off ramp in the war by negotiating a nuclear deal with Iran. Trump, who had been breathing down the Netanyahu's neck to restrain from hostilites, in a recent interview spoke on how he "calls the shot", affirming that Netanyahu must accept the Iran deal to put cessation to the bloody war.

articles.title: ‘Keep Chai In Cup, Idli On Plate’: Shashi Tharoor's Witty Response To Woman's Bizarre Food Combo
articles.description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor has once again stepped forward to defend humble idli, this time weighing in on a viral social media post that hailed "chai and idli" as the "best combination to ever exist."

articles.title: Why Iran-US Peace Talks Take A Hit Every Time Israel Strikes Lebanon
articles.description: Iran fired missiles towards Israel on Sunday after Israeli strikes in Lebanon, dramatically testing a ceasefire that US President Donald Trump had been trying to preserve.

articles.title: After Beirut bombed again, Iran warns Israel to 'watch the sky tonight' as fears of retaliation grow
articles.description: Tensions surged after Israel launched fresh strikes on Beirut’s Dahiyeh suburb, prompting a dramatic warning from Iran. Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Rezaei vowed a “decisive and painful” response and urged Israelis to “watch the sky tonight,” fueling fears of imminent retaliation. The threat came after the I.D.F. said it targeted Hezbollah infrastructure following rocket fire from Lebanon. As residents fled parts of Beirut fearing more bombardments, concerns grew that the fragile ceasefire framework could collapse. With attacks continuing across southern Lebanon, the risk of a wider regional confrontation is once again rising sharply.

articles.title: News in Pics | June 8, 2026: Best Photos from around the world
TDP finalises 3 names for Upper House
articles.description: The Telugu Desam Party has selected three candidates for the upcoming Rajya Sabha elections. Sana Sathish Babu will be renominated. Other chosen candidates are educationist Bhashyam Ramakrishna and Vijay Chinthakayala, son of Andhra Pradesh Speaker Chinthakayala Ayyana Patrudu. The NDA alliance secured a majority in the assembly elections, enabling them to claim four Rajya Sabha seats.

articles.title: J&K LG flags off 'Nasha Mukt' padyatra, urges collective action
articles.description: J&K LG Manoj Sinha flagged off the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan padyatra in Poonch, urging collective action against drug abuse. The campaign aims to raise awareness, strengthen preventive measures, and ensure community participation in the fight against addiction.

articles.description: Kolkata: Around 4,800 people have been pushed to Bangladesh and another 836 people are in holding centers, CM Suvendu Adhikari said on Sunday at a BJP.

articles.title: 5 rebel TMC Birbhum MLAs get security upgrades
articles.description: Bolpur/Kolkata: Bengal govt on Sunday upgraded security of five Trinamool MLAs, all of whom are part of the Ritabrata Banerjee-led rebel camp of 58 ML.

articles.title: Iran's missile attack, Israel's retaliation warning, Trump says ‘enough’: Latest in US-Iran war
articles.description: Israel has reportedly said that it will retaliate against Iranian attacks. Meanwhile, Iran also said it will respond to any Israeli attack "with greater force".
